Hi Annia, If you set the schedule to apply penalties every day for all cycles, the system will only apply penalties to the accounts that meet the criteria for the penalty. You should test this before setting in live though.
Hi Annia, I sent out a tentative meeting invite. Please suggest another time if tomorrow at 3pm your time doesn't work for you.
Hi Annia, We will need to set up a time to review all the open tickets with you and Jhony in January.
Hi Annia,

Dear DL-UBCS-TS, The resolution for this ticket has been provided. If the issue has not been resolved please update the ticket as soon as possible by responding in this ticket. (Please note: You have up to 7 days to notify inHANCE of any issues or concerns related to this ticket. If no issues or concerns have been noted within the 7-day window, the issue is considered complete and this ticket will automatically close.) However, if the issue has been resolved, please close the ticket and take a moment to provide your rating of the service you received.

Hi Annie, Yes, in the task scheduler under System Administration > System Configuration > Task Scheduler. Create a new task, select Penalties as the Task type. Enter a Code and Description, select your penalty group, Increment times penalized or not and the Due Dt Offset. Then select the cycle or cycles and save. If it complains, you may need to select a User to notify. Save the changes, then edit the schedule and save again. This creates a SQL job that runs on the schedule you select (or single execution if you selected that option). Be aware that this will run even if your normal billing is off by a few days. So if you bill on a specific day and run penalties directly after and you are running a little late on billing, you may need to adjust the schedule. Please let us know if you have any questions about how to set this up.
Mackenzie, We haven't heard from you for some time. If this issue persists, please reopen this ticket or a new one.
Hi Mackenzie, Please let us know if the information below answered your questions on this ticket.

Hi Mackenzie, When payments come into the system, they get distributed according to your payment priority usually, unless you have some customization. I don't see anything in the batch payment screen that would indicate a specific set of income centers a payment can be sent. Even if you had two sets of files, one for water and one for sewer, there is nothing to indicate to only pay one income center or the other. Do you know if you have a customization or if you used the customer screen's payment process in the past? The customer's screen's payment process will allow you to indicate specific income centers, however, that is a manual process.

Hello, I figured out the import file. My next question is, is there a way to tell inhance where you want the payments applied? One of my files is for water and one is for sewer. These amounts are for anything billed and due before June 30 of the previous year. In the past the payments applied to the correct charges, but it didn’t seem to apply to the correct charges this year…
